subject: fix(db): make spatial_ref_sys RLS migration idempotent
body:
The prior version errored on re-run with "policy already exists" and
"duplicate key value" on the schema_migrations INSERT. If a paste into
Studio partially succeeded (ALTER ok, CREATE POLICY failed for any
reason), the user couldn't safely re-run the same block.

Changes to the migration file:
- DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before CREATE POLICY so re-runs don't error on
  the policy name. ENABLE RLS is already a no-op when already enabled.
- Header note explaining the ownership constraint (supabase_admin vs
  postgres) and the Studio SQL Editor escape hatch.

Changes to USER_ACTIONS.md:
- Inlined the full SQL block (including the ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
  ledger insert) so the user runs one paste, not two — prior version
  sent them to open the migration file + a separate INSERT snippet.
- Stronger verification query (checks RLS flag, policy count, and
  ledger row in one call).
- Documented the platform-change fallback (if Studio also errors 42501,
  open a support ticket — don't retry via CLI).

Tags: database, migrations, supabase, security, idempotency
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: db-sync
Semver: patch
title: make spatial_ref_sys RLS migration idempotent
